9:00 AM – Patient Appointment 1: Athlete with Chronic Knee Pain (Suspected Meniscus Tear)  
A 28-year-old soccer player presents with persistent medial knee pain after a twisting injury 6 weeks ago. The chiropractor performs a thorough orthopedic assessment: palpation of joint lines, McMurray’s test, and gait analysis. They rule out ligamentous instability and confirm mechanical symptoms consistent with a degenerative meniscus tear. Using the Activator Protocol, they perform low-force, high-velocity adjustments to the tibiofemoral joint, patellofemoral articulation, and adjacent lumbar segments to reduce nerve irritation and improve joint mechanics.
